examples.title: Fluoride Concentration Check Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to determine water safety based on fluoride concentration
examples.code:
FUNCTION CheckFluorideSafety(fluorideLevel)
 // fluorideLevel in mg/L
 
 IF fluorideLevel < 0.5 THEN
 RETURN "DEFICIENT - Risk of dental caries"
 ELSE IF fluorideLevel >= 0.5 AND fluorideLevel <= 1.5 THEN
 RETURN "OPTIMAL - Safe for consumption"
 ELSE IF fluorideLevel > 1.5 AND fluorideLevel <= 4.0 THEN
 RETURN "HIGH - Risk of dental fluorosis"
 ELSE
 RETURN "DANGEROUS - Risk of skeletal fluorosis"
 END IF
END FUNCTION

// Example usage
INPUT fluorideConcentration = 2.8
OUTPUT = CheckFluorideSafety(fluorideConcentration)
// Output: "HIGH - Risk of dental fluorosis"
examples.explanation: This algorithm categorizes water based on fluoride concentration into four zones: deficient (caries risk), optimal (healthy), high (dental fluorosis risk), and dangerous (skeletal fluorosis risk).

examples.title: Nalgonda Defluoridation Process
examples.description: Step-by-step procedure for Nalgonda technique defluoridation
examples.code:
PROCEDURE NalgondaDefluoridation(waterVolume, fluorideLevel)
 // Calculate required chemicals
 alumDose = waterVolume * 0.001 // 1 mg alum per liter
 limeDose = waterVolume * 0.0005 // 0.5 mg lime per liter
 
 // Add alum and lime to water
 ADD alumDose TO waterTank
 ADD limeDose TO waterTank
 
 // Stir for 15 minutes
 FOR i = 1 TO 15
 STIR continuously
 END FOR
 
 // Allow flocs to settle (2 hours)
 WAIT 120 minutes
 
 // Decant clear water
 DECANT upperClearLayer
 
 // Remaining fluoride check
 newFluorideLevel = TEST_FLUORIDE(decantedWater)
 
 IF newFluorideLevel > 1.5 THEN
 REPEAT process with adjusted dose
 END IF
 
 RETURN decantedWater
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: The Nalgonda technique involves adding alum (aluminum sulfate) and lime to water, which react with fluoride to form insoluble aluminum fluoride flocs that settle down, leaving clear water with reduced fluoride.

examples.title: Activated Alumina Defluoridation Cycle
examples.description: Shows adsorption and regeneration cycle of activated alumina
examples.code:
// Phase 1: Adsorption (Fluoride Removal)
PROCEDURE AdsorptionCycle(influentFluoride)
 aluminaBed = InitializeActivatedAlumina()
 
 WHILE aluminaBed.isNotSaturated() DO
 waterFlow = PUMP_IN(influentFluoride)
 fluorideAdsorbed = aluminaBed.catchFluoride(waterFlow)
 effluent = waterFlow - fluorideAdsorbed
 IF effluent.fluoride > 1.5 THEN
 ALERT "Bed approaching saturation"
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 RETURN "Regeneration Required"
END PROCEDURE

// Phase 2: Regeneration
PROCEDURE RegenerationCycle()
 // Wash with 1-2% NaOH solution
 NaOH_FLOW = 2.0 // percent solution
 CONTACT_TIME = 45 minutes
 
 // Remove adsorbed fluoride
 BACKWASH with NaOH_FLOW for CONTACT_TIME
 
 // Rinse with water
 FINAL_RINSE with clean water
 
 RETURN aluminaBed.isReady()
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Activated alumina works by adsorbing fluoride ions. When saturated, it can be regenerated using sodium hydroxide solution to restore its adsorption capacity.
